raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The IGS Poll finds that among the 87 percent of Californians with a home high-speed Internet connection, 18 percent have access through a smartphone only.",
    "69 percent of Californians report having broadband Internet access through a computing device.",
    "The most disadvantaged residents remain offline and 'underconnected.'",
    "69 percent of those without Internet access at home cite broadband service expense or not owning a computer or smartphone as a reason for not being connected.",
    "34 percent of individuals without Internet access say that broadband service expense is their main reason for not being connected.",
    "44 percent of respondents without Internet access cite home Internet as too difficult to set up and learn, indicating a need for adoption assistance and digital literacy training.",
    "19 percent of respondents report that Internet service is not available where they live, indicating broadband infrastructure problems.",
    "An April 2016 report of the California Public Utilities Commission found that 43 percent of rural California households can’t get reliable broadband Internet."
  ]
}
```
